title: Data Stores Mapping & Schema Normalization
description: Added comprehensive support for Make.com Data Stores, including deployment mapping, schema mismatch detection, and Diff Viewer integration.

changes.message: Added a 'Hide Data Store Difference' toggle to the Settings Sidebar, allowing users to optionally filter out Data Store ID changes from the comparison report.

Robust Data Store Schema Extraction & Pruning: The deployment engine now uses a multi-tier fallback system to extract Data Store schemas (including parsing the raw blueprint metadata.expect cache) to safely strip orphaned fields before pushing payloads to production.
Added Data Store Mapping: Users can now map Sandbox data stores to Production data stores directly in the deployment modal, complete with a dedicated 'Stores' tab and database icons.
Data Store Mismatch Warnings: The engine now fetches and deeply compares the underlying Data Structure schemas of mapped Data Stores, alerting users with caution flags if the schemas differ.
Human-Readable Data Stores: The Diff Viewer now dynamically looks up and displays the actual names of Data Stores instead of raw Make.com IDs.

title: Data Structures Mapping & Safe Payload Pruning
description: Introduced full support for Make.com Data Structures, including deployment mapping, schema mismatch detection, and safe pruning of orphaned fields.

changes.message: Added Data Structure Mapping: Users can now map Sandbox data structures to Production data structures directly in the deployment modal alongside Connections and Webhooks.
Data Structure Mismatch Warnings: The engine now deeply compares Data Structure specs (using lodash) and alerts users with caution flags if the mapped structures have divergent schemas.

Safe Pruning of Orphaned Fields: The deployment engine now recursively scans and removes fields from the JSON payload if they exist in the Sandbox structure but are missing in the Production structure, preventing dirty data injection.

Resolved a bug where Make.com's stale visual cache (metadata.restore) caused the Differ to display incorrect Data Structure names post-deployment. Sceniform now forcefully injects the true API names during the deployment push.
Added a 'Hide Data Structure Difference' toggle to the Settings Sidebar to let users ignore underlying structural ID changes while preserving actual mapping visibility.

title: Smarter Deployments & Modal Refactor
description: Introduced a cleaner tabbed layout for environment bindings, upgraded the connection auto-mapping engine, and squashed a connection detection bug.

changes.message: Redesigned the Deployment Modal with a tabbed navigation system to distinctly separate Connections, Hooks, Structures, and Stores.
Upgraded the auto-mapping engine to use a 3-tier fallback system. Sceniform now intelligently maps connections by matching Module IDs, exact Connection IDs, or App Types between environments.
Fixed an extraction bug where certain connections (e.g., standard Google/Gmail modules) were not detected if Make.com stored their IDs without the standard '__IMTCONN__' prefix.

title: Sceniform MVP Launch
description: The official public release of the Sceniform engine, bringing secure, side-by-side Make.com blueprint diffing to automation agencies.

changes.message: Introduced Connection Mapping: Dynamically map and verify environment connections (e.g., Sandbox to Production) before confirming a deployment.
Implemented enterprise-grade API key storage and multi-tenant workspace isolation for distinct organizations and teams.
Added active/inactive scenario indicators and terminal-style error handling directly linked to the source scenarios.

title: Complex Routing & Merge Module Support
description: Major upgrades to the DAG parsing engine to handle complex scenario architectures and edge cases.

changes.message: Added deep support for if/else logic and Merge modules. The Differ now correctly tracks variables across unified paths after a Router split.
Fixed a cascading diff issue where child modules were incorrectly flagged as changed when only the parent module was updated.
Interactive Tracing: Clicking on mapped variables now accurately auto-scrolls to the origin module, even across deeply nested graphs.

title: Mapping Readability & Engine Performance
description: Enhancements to how variable mappings are displayed and backend rendering performance.

changes.message: Human-Readable Mappings: The UI now parses and displays human-readable labels in mappings instead of raw Make.com variable IDs.
Added secure backend proxy caching (EF Core) for third-party module logos.

title: Alpha: Core DAG Visualization
description: The initial build of the Sceniform differ engine and vertical graph layout.

changes.message: Router Analysis: The engine now visually distinguishes between main routes, collapsing router branches, and explicitly identifies disabled routes.
Introduced the side-by-side blueprint viewer with intelligent indentation.
